subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2026, World Bank, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, OHCHR, UNHCR, TI); Angolan state media EXCLUDED there, with economics admitted via IMF.
A near-total oil rentier under a party that has ruled since 1975, with high inflation, heavy China-debt service, and extreme inequality.
Standout drags: a resource-curse oil model, weak rule of law, high inflation, abusive migrant expulsions, and weak healthcare; a US-courted Lobito Corridor is a mild geopolitical offset.

economic.notes: A near-total oil rentier with heavy China-debt service, a volatile currency, and high inflation.

fiscal_state.notes: A ~4% deficit with debt ~52% rising toward the legal ceiling, heavy China oil-backed debt service, and an IMF-program history. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An oil-managed kwanza with a ~60% 2023 devaluation and limited central-bank autonomy. (IMF; FocusEconomics)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: ~95% of exports from oil with structurally declining output and minimal diversification - a classic resource curse. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

institutional.notes: MPLA dominance with no judicial independence and a state-dominated press.

rule_of_law.notes: Party dominance with no judicial independence, a grand-corruption legacy, and only a partial/selective anti-graft drive. (Freedom House; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak-ish but actively IMF-engaged and validated. (IMF)

press_freedom.notes: A poor RSF standing, with a 2024 security law allowing media/internet interference and ongoing prosecutions. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A half-century of one-party rule, restricted liberties, abusive migrant expulsions, and extreme inequality.

political_stability.notes: A party in power since 1975, a contested 2022 election, and rising discontent ahead of a 2027 vote - competitive-authoritarian managed stability. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: A 2025 anti-'vandalism' law (long terms for protests) and crackdowns. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a relocation destination, with recurrent abusive mass expulsions of Congolese migrants. (HRW; OHCHR; UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Extreme inequality and high poverty despite oil, with a Cabinda separatist undercurrent. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a growth dividend against an acute jobs strain. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A pragmatic China/US-hedging posture, with low-level Cabinda separatism.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned, China-debt-tied yet US-courted via the Lobito Corridor - pragmatic hedging. (ECFR; AidData)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war, with low-level Cabinda separatism and DRC-border exposure. (HRW)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: FX controls present; not internationally sanctioned. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Weak healthcare and infrastructure, against drought/flood and high urban crime.

climate.notes: High vulnerability/low readiness; recurrent severe southern drought plus coastal flood/erosion. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Low spend, a high disease burden, and a weak system. (World Bank/WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Oil-funded pockets (the Lobito rail) but broadly poor, with only ~47% reliable power access. (World Bank; Afrobarometer)

summary: A near-total oil rentier - roughly 95% of exports from oil with structurally declining output - run since 1975 by a single party, with heavy China-backed debt service, a volatile kwanza, and high, sticky inflation. Institutions offer no judicial independence, a grand-corruption legacy with only a selective anti-graft drive, and a state-dominated press, while extreme inequality persists despite the oil wealth and migrants face recurrent abusive mass expulsions. Weak healthcare and infrastructure, drought/flood exposure, and high urban crime weigh on the physical side; a US-courted Lobito Corridor is a mild geopolitical offset.
